_comment: Single source of truth for the game version shown on the main menu. Newest release first. When you make a player-facing change, PREPEND a new entry (bump the version, set today's date, write a short summary + change bullets). See CLAUDE.md 'Version / changelog' for the convention.

releases.summary: Unit colors now display as a ring by default instead of filling the whole base, and duplicate squads of the same unit are automatically given different colors. The new Settings > Visual 'Unit Color' option defaults to 'Ring Only', which keeps a neutral base and paints your chosen color as a ring inside the base edge so the model art stays clear — switch it to 'Full Base' for the original solid-base look. Deploying a second squad of the same name (e.g. a second Boyz) now auto-picks a different color from the first.
changes: Unit colors now show as a ring around the model by default (neutral base, color band just inside the base perimeter) so the model art stays readable.
New Settings > Visual dropdown 'Unit Color' — 'Ring Only' (default) or 'Full Base' (the original solid colored base).
Right-click > Change Color still sets the color; the setting only changes whether it fills the base or shows as a ring. Applies live to every token, no reload needed, and persists between sessions.
Duplicate squads of the same unit type/name now get distinct auto-assigned colors as you deploy them (fixes a bug where every squad came out the same color).
Takes effect in the default 'Letter' unit style where the token color is used.

releases.summary: Burden of Trust guards can now be nominated straight from the board: each objective row in the 'Assign Guards' dialog has a 'Pick on board' button — click it, then click one of your units on the battlefield to make it that objective's guard. The dropdown stays as an alternative.
changes: New 'Pick on board' button on every objective row: it hides the dialog, shows a banner, and the next click on one of your units assigns it as that objective's guard, then re-opens the dialog with the pick applied.
A 'Cancel' button on the banner (and clicking empty space / a non-eligible unit) returns you to the dialog without changing anything.
The dropdown selection and 'Pick on board' stay in sync, so you can mix and match; Confirm Guards / Keep Auto Picks work exactly as before.

releases.summary: The Burden of Trust guard dropdown now names each unit the same way as the roster panel — including the Alpha/Beta suffix and the model count — so two 'Boyz' squads (or two Warbosses) are no longer indistinguishable.
changes: Guard dropdown entries now read e.g. 'Boyz Alpha (20 models, in range)' instead of a bare 'Boyz', so you can tell duplicate squads apart when nominating a guard.
The '(in range)' / '(embarked)' hint is now folded into the same parenthetical as the model count for a cleaner, single-line label.

releases.summary: Burden of Trust guard selection now lets you pick ANY of your units to guard an objective, not just the single highest-priority one it auto-picked. The dropdown lists every friendly unit on the battlefield, marks the ones currently in range (they score right now), and shows a row for every objective.
changes: Fixed: the 'Assign Guards' dropdown previously offered only one unit per objective, so a unit sitting on the objective (e.g. Gretchin Beta) could be missing — you can now choose any friendly unit, matching the card ('select one friendly unit per objective to guard it').
Units currently within range of the objective are tagged '(in range)' and listed first, so you can see at a glance which picks would score at the end of your opponent's turn.
The prompt now shows a row for every objective on the board (not only ones you already have a unit on), so you can pre-assign a guard for an objective you are about to capture.
Embarked units are tagged '(embarked)'; scoring is unchanged (a guarded objective still only scores while its guard is in range and you control it).

releases.summary: Fixed the AI abandoning most of its moves. The AI planned moves its own rules engine then rejected, so unit after unit gave up and stayed in the deployment zone. It now checks the same rules the game enforces before committing, so its units actually advance.
changes: AI no longer routes moves through Monster/Vehicle bases it can't cross — it modelled big rectangular bases (e.g. the Battlewagon) as a too-small circle and clipped their ends, so every such move was thrown out. It now uses the exact base shape and paths around them.

AI moves now keep unit coherency: when a model had to shuffle around an obstacle it could end up too far from its squad, so the whole move was rejected at confirm time. Models are now relocated only to spots that keep the unit together.
AI no longer stacks models on top of each other in tight spaces (its 'squeeze in' fallback could overlap bases the game forbids).

Net effect: far fewer units freeze in place, so the AI presses objectives and plays the board instead of standing still. Also fixed a harmless script error that fired when the AI reset or undid a move.

releases.summary: The Scoring phase now spells out each player's primary mission in full: alongside the card name you get the official scoring blurb and a plain-English, per-rule VP breakdown, so you can see exactly how your primary scores without a rulebook.
changes: Primary mission panel (Scoring phase): each player's disposition-paired card now shows a 'PRIMARY MISSIONS' section with the card title, the official mission text, and a bullet list of every scoring rule — e.g. 'End of game: +10VP — control the enemy's home objective'.

Each rule line reads its timing (Command phase / End of turn / End of game), the battle-round window (R2+, R1–2, ...), the VP, and the condition, generated from the same rules the game actually scores so the text can't drift from the maths.
Card names are colour-coded per player and cards built from approximate rules are still marked with a '~'.
No change to how primaries score — this is display only.

releases.summary: Long game-log entries — like the AI's move rationale — are no longer silently cut off. A truncated entry now shows a clear 'show more' button you can click to read the full text, and 'show less' to collapse it again.
changes: Game Log: entries longer than the preview length now render a visible 'show more' / 'show less' toggle instead of trailing off with an unlabelled '…'. Clicking the entry text also expands it.

Raised the preview length so typical one-line AI decision rationales (e.g. 'Prosecutors moves toward obj_center …') display in full with no clicking needed; only genuinely long entries get the toggle.
Expanding a long entry is now reversible, so reading it no longer permanently grows the log.

releases.summary: Hold X to check line of sight to a spot before you commit to it: every model that can see the mouse position lights up with a gold sight line — perfect for asking 'if I move here, who can see me?' The tool existed but was unreachable because its keys (V, then G) were taken over by other shortcuts.
changes: Hold X ('X marks the spot'): a crosshair marks the cursor position and every model with line of sight to it — yours AND the enemy's — draws a gold sighting line with a highlight ring and unit name (blue inner ring = player 1, red = player 2). Release to clear.

Use it while planning a move: hover the destination (it also works mid-drag) and instantly see which enemies would have line of sight to a model standing there, and what it could see out to (72" range).

The probe tracks the cursor by polling too, so it keeps updating even when panels swallow mouse-move events; the check is base-edge aware and follows the same terrain rules as shooting (ruins, obscuring, walls, heights).
Holding L in the shooting phase keeps its overlay through phase-entry housekeeping now, whether a shooter was auto-armed (per-model debug) or not (unit-to-unit overview).

Fixed the tool being unreachable: its key handling moved into the main input handler as the rebindable 'Check Line of Sight (hold)' action (Settings → Controls), defaulting to X — the old V and G bindings (and the parallel los_check registration on G) were consumed first by the VP timeline and tactical grid toggles, so it never fired. The '?' shortcut overlay lists it.

releases.summary: Added a Settings toggle to hide terrain scatter props. Settings > Visual now has a 'Terrain Scatter Props (crates, sandbags, trees)' checkbox — turn it off for a cleaner, less cluttered board that shows just the terrain footprints, borders and walls. On by default; the choice is saved between sessions.
changes: New Settings > Visual checkbox 'Terrain Scatter Props (crates, sandbags, trees)' — toggles the decorative props scattered on terrain pieces.
Takes effect immediately on the board (no reload needed) and persists across sessions.
When off, terrain still shows its footprints, borders, objective markers and walls — only the cosmetic scatter is hidden.

releases.summary: Removed the red barrel props from ruins terrain. Viewed top-down they read as flat red circles that looked like objective or blast markers rather than scenery, so ruins now scatter only crates and sandbags.
changes: Ruins terrain no longer scatters the top-down barrel sprite (the red circles); crates and sandbags remain.

releases.summary: Every unit in the two default armies now has top-down artwork. All 12 units across Recon Stomps (Orks) and Custodes Lions (Adeptus Custodes) render bird's-eye sprites on their bases — including the Stompa's full effigy hull, rotor-spinning Deffkoptas and Warbikes that fill their oval bases, and gold Allarus Terminators.
changes: New ork art: Stompa (effigy face, mega-choppa + supa-gatler arms, hazard stripes on the 180mm base), Deffkilla Wartrike, Deffkoptas, Warbikers, Wazdakka Gutsmek, Stormboyz (rokkit pack + flame), Mek (giant spanner), and Gretchin (big-eared grots).

New Custodes art: Allarus Custodians (terminator bulk with castellan axe), Blade Champion (red cloak + longsword), and Prosecutors (black Sisters of Silence armour with gold trim); Custodian Guard shipped in 0.34.0.
Bike, kopta and wartrike sprites are drawn on tall canvases that fill their oval bases and rotate with the model's facing.

Units with dedicated artwork now show it even if they are vehicles — the generic tank sprite remains the fallback for vehicles without their own art (e.g. the Battlewagon), and letter counters remain for everything else.
Player re-skins still override: drop a PNG named after the unit (e.g. stompa.png) into user://sprites/.

releases.summary: First top-down unit artwork: Custodian Guard squads now show a hand-drawn bird's-eye Custodian (gold armour, red helm crest, guardian spear) on their bases instead of a letter counter — the start of Vassal-style sprites for every model in the game.
changes: New: top-down Custodian Guard sprite — tokens for any unit named 'Custodian Guard' render the gold spear-wielding figure on their base in the default (letter) visual style; the spear overhangs the base edge just like the tabletop model.
Units without artwork are unchanged and keep their letter counters (and vehicles keep their tank sprites); the art also appears in the 'enhanced' visual style.
Custom re-skins still work: a PNG dropped into user://sprites/ with the same name (custodian_guard.png) overrides the built-in art.
More sprites are on the way — the art pipeline (tools/sprites/generate_unit_sprites.py) is built to cover the rest of the Adeptus Custodes range next.

releases.summary: Wazdakka Gutsmek now sits on his correct 120mm x 92mm oval base instead of a 32mm round one, so his footprint, movement, engagement range and charges match the tabletop model.
changes: Wazdakka Gutsmek's base is now a 120mm x 92mm oval (previously a 32mm round base), matching his physical Warhammer 40,000 model.
This affects his board footprint, base-to-base contact, engagement range and how the token renders and rotates.

releases.summary: Model carry — the heart of controller play: with a unit selected, press A to pick its model up onto the cursor, steer it with the left stick (slower, precise speed while carrying), A drops it, LB/RB rotates it, D-pad left/right hops between the unit's models, B cancels back to where it was picked up, and X undoes the last placed model. Works in the Movement phase and Deployment (including transport embark prompts).
changes: Movement phase: A (with the cursor parked) picks up the active model — the real drag rules run underneath, so move caps, terrain, overlaps and unit coherency are enforced exactly as with the mouse; A drops and stages the move, Confirm Move commits it.
D-pad left/right hops between the selected unit's models so multi-model squads can be moved model by model without hunting; X undoes the last staged model.
LB/RB rotates the carried model (oval and large bases) — the same rebindable rotate keys as Q/E, so custom bindings apply on pad too.

Deployment: A jumps the cursor (and the placement ghost) into your deployment zone, A places each model, LB/RB rotates the ghost, and transport prompts like 'Embark Units in Battlewagon' get pad focus.
While carrying, the stick uses a slower precision speed — inch budgets are small and overshooting past the move cap was too easy at cursor speed.
B now returns you fully to the board in one press (releases panel focus AND parks the cursor) — previously it could take two presses and the next A could misfire as a click.
Fixed: controller focus could not reach buttons created inside a dialog's built-in button row (e.g. 'Confirm Embarkation') — dialog focus and button lookups now see them.

releases.summary: Native controller controls arrive: LB/RB cycles your units (the camera jumps to each one), the shooting phase is fully playable without the cursor — cycle eligible shooters, cycle eligible targets, A assigns, Start confirms — Y opens the datasheet, X skips the active shooter, a D-pad press hops into the side panels and B hops back out. The button hints at the bottom now change with what you're doing.
changes: LB/RB cycle: in most phases the bumpers step through the unit list (exactly like clicking rows); in the shooting phase they step through eligible shooters and — once a shooter is armed — through its eligible targets, with the camera panning to whatever is highlighted.

Native shooting: A assigns the highlighted target to the current weapon, X skips the active shooter, B deselects, and Start becomes 'Confirm Targets' while assignments are pending (it stays 'End Phase' otherwise, still behind a confirmation).
Y toggles the datasheet of the highlighted target or selected unit.
D-pad with nothing focused enters the side panels (unit list first) so every panel button is reachable without the cursor; B releases focus back to the board.
The controller hint bar is now contextual — it shows cycling/assign hints while targeting, navigation hints while in panels, and the standard set on the board.
Wound-allocation prompts now receive controller focus like dialogs do, so A presses Finish/Continue there too.
Fixed: the very first controller press of a session could be swallowed while the game still considered the mouse active.

releases.summary: The whole game is now playable with a controller: the left stick drives a virtual cursor that clicks and drags anything the mouse can (select units, drag models, press any button), pushing the cursor against the screen edge pans the camera, the Menu button ends the phase behind a confirm dialog, and every dialog that pops up lands controller focus on its confirm button so A proceeds and B backs out.
changes: Virtual cursor: the left stick moves a visible pointer that behaves exactly like the mouse — A is left-click (hold A to drag models), X is right-click. Everything reachable by mouse is now reachable by pad, in every phase.
Edge-push camera: driving the cursor against the edge of the screen pans the board (standard RTS behaviour), so off-screen units are reachable with the stick alone.

Cursor vs focus: moving the stick gives the buttons to the cursor; touching the D-pad (or any dialog opening) hands them back to focus navigation — so one press can never click the board AND press a focused button at once.
Menu (Start) button ends the current phase behind an 'End X Phase?' confirmation — A confirms, B cancels.
Dialogs are controller-first now: any dialog that appears while playing on pad focuses its confirm button (not 'Go Back'), so A means proceed and B dismisses; the Settings overlay also closes on B.
A full solo turn — command, movement (moving a model by cursor drag), shooting, charge, fight and scoring, into the next player's turn — is verified played entirely without mouse or keyboard.
Fixed: controller input was invisible to the input-device tracker when a dialog was open or a handler consumed the event first — glyph hints and cursor availability now switch reliably.

releases.summary: Fixed a Fight phase bug where one of your units in combat could be skipped entirely when playing against the AI. If the AI reached a point where it couldn't select one of its own units, it would end the whole Fight phase — forfeiting every remaining fight, including yours (e.g. a Stompa in base contact that never got to swing).
changes: Fight phase: the list of units offered the 'select to fight' action is now driven by the real 11th-edition fight sequencer, tagged with the correct player, instead of a legacy queue that drifted out of order once fights happened out of sequence. Your engaged units are now always offered their fight in the right order.

AI: when the AI can't select a fighter it now re-checks the fight order and continues, instead of ending the entire Fight phase and forfeiting every remaining fight (yours included). It only ends the fight when the sequencer confirms no unit on either side is still eligible.
AI: the AI no longer tries to pick your units to fight during the alternating fight sequence — it only considers selection offers addressed to it.

releases.summary: Removed the legacy hand-made terrain layouts (Chapter Approved 1-8 and the parse-test boards). The game now plays exclusively on the converted official 11e tournament layouts, and the main menu defaults to the current matchup's first official variant with its paired deployment.
changes: Removed: the ten legacy terrain layouts (Chapter Approved Layouts 1-8 and two parse-test boards) and their hardcoded fallback. They pre-dated the official 11e set, used whole-rectangle footprints with hand-authored wall segments, and interacted badly with the new solid-wall movement rules.
The terrain dropdown now lists only the official 11e layouts: the selected Force-Disposition matchup's three variants (or the full official list if no matchup applies).

New games default to the default matchup's first official variant (Take and Hold mirror, variant 1) with its paired deployment pattern (Tipping Point) — previously the default was a legacy parse-test board with Search and Destroy.

Old saves and settings that reference a removed layout still load: saved games rebuild their terrain from the data stored in the save itself, and a stale layout selection falls back to the default official layout instead of an empty board.

releases.summary: Ruin walls now actually block vehicles and monsters. Big models (like the Stompa) can no longer walk through, charge through, or park on top of the solid walls on the tournament terrain layouts — and the whole base counts, not just its centre point.
changes: Fixed: vehicles and monsters could move straight through — and end their move standing on — the 5" ruin walls on the official-style (11e) terrain layouts. The dense-terrain movement rule was only enforced on an internal action the drag UI and the AI never used, and the 'cannot end overlapping a wall' rule only knew about the old hand-authored wall segments, which the new layouts don't have.

Movement legality is now shape-aware: the model's full base is swept along its path and checked at the destination, so a wide base (e.g. the Stompa's 180mm) can no longer straddle a wall its centre line never crossed.
Charge moves are checked too — a non-Fly vehicle can no longer charge through a ruin wall (Fly units still charge over terrain; nobody may END a charge inside a wall).

Infantry are unaffected — they still move through and stand among ruin walls freely, and step-over allowances still work: units that ignore terrain up to 4" (Stompa's Stompin' Forward, the Nauts' Clankin' Forward, SUPER-HEAVY WALKER) can still cross features up to that height, just not 5" walls.
Ruin footprints (the outlined areas around walls) remain enterable through their openings — only the solid wall pieces themselves block.
The AI now routes around walls instead of parking on them, and screens its candidate moves with the same rule the engine enforces.
Models left standing inside a wall by an older save can always move OUT of it — they just can't stay or go further in.

releases.summary: Every keyboard shortcut can now be remapped mid-game. Settings → Controls now lists ALL of the game's hotkeys (previously most were hardcoded and missing from the menu), grouped into Camera, Gameplay, Model, Panels & Overlays, AI, Debug and Replay Playback — click any binding and press a new key to change it, and it takes effect immediately.
changes: Added ~40 previously-unlisted shortcuts to the Controls tab so they can be reassigned: Army panel (U), Stratagems (S), Missions (M), Weapon range (W), Datasheet (I), Chat (T), Roster strip (B), Aura rings (A), Tactical grid (G), VP timeline (V), Visual style (8), Fit-to-board (F) / Fit-to-selection (Shift+F), Ruler (R), Threat overlay (Tab), Line-of-sight overlays (L / hold-G check), the AI replay + playback controls, and the debug tools (9 / O / Shift+D / F10).

Every one of these keys now flows through the rebinding system, so changing it in Settings → Controls changes it in the actual game — no restart needed. Rebinds are saved and persist between sessions.
Bindings are organised into clear categories with a Reset (per-key) and Reset All Defaults button, and the on-screen '?' hotkey help now shows your current keys instead of the factory defaults.

Note: several shortcuts still SHIP on the same default key as another (e.g. W is both 'pan up' and 'weapon range'; G is terrain / grid / LoS-check) — they are now all visible in the menu so you can give each one its own key.

releases.summary: The AI's game log now opens each turn with a single 'turn intent' card — its stance for the round (e.g. AGGRESSIVE / MELEE army), what the round is worth in VP, which enemies it plans to charge or tie up, and every unit's orders. Fast melee units (Stormboyz, Custodian Guard) also deploy like assault troops now instead of lining up as gunline shooters.
changes: Game log: the movement-plan card is now a full turn-intent briefing — round stance (strategy + army archetype), 'Playing for:' VP stakes, named charge intents ("Charge intent: Stormboyz → Custodian Guard (20.8\" away)"), and shooters the AI plans to tie up in melee, followed by the per-unit orders.

AI deployment: units whose weapons make them melee-focused (Stormboyz with token pistols, Custodian Guard with guardian spears) now classify as melee and deploy forward for movement lanes; previously any unit carrying a ranged weapon deployed as a 'ranged unit positioning for firing lanes'. Uses the same melee-focus gate as the movement/charge logic so deployment and battle plans agree about a unit's role.
Fixed: secondary missions requiring a unit action are now scored impossible when the player has no units left.

releases.summary: The AI now fights to a single battle plan per turn — and the game log tells the truth about it. The movement plan the AI announces is the plan its units actually execute, melee hunts are declared up front ("ATTACK Custodian Guard"), spare units spread across objectives instead of piling onto one, and every decision card explains WHY (including when coordination overrides the raw scores).
changes: AI movement: the army-wide unit-to-objective plan is computed once per movement phase and then followed, instead of being re-solved before every single unit action (which made announced plans and executed moves contradict each other). Re-plans only happen for cause (e.g. a unit disembarks mid-phase) and are called out in the log.

AI coordination: leftover units now spread across objectives with diminishing returns instead of all independently picking the same marker; objectives one enemy move away from being contested get sized reinforcement ("capturing it — enemy can bring ~4 OC next move").
AI melee: chase-vs-objective decisions are made at planning time and announced in the battle plan as ATTACK entries; the objective a hunter abandons re-opens for another unit to backfill.

Game log honesty: per-unit decision cards now show the REAL assignment with its reason (capture / flip / reinforce / sole holder / attack), and when the chosen option scores lower than a rejected one the card says why ("↳ why not the top score: obj_1 is already covered..."). Previously ~58% of cards claimed a 'chosen' option that contradicted the action taken.

AI formations: reserve declarations respect board presence — armies no longer bank nearly half their points in Strategic Reserves and concede the early objective game (the Custodes Lions list kept 950pts off-board; now capped near 25%).
Fixed: the phase banner and AI Actions overlay always showed 'Round 1' — they now show the actual battle round.

Fixed: a game-freezing crash in the shooting phase — when a weapon's single auto-assigned target was rejected (or its processing refreshed the weapon panel mid-update), the UI re-entered itself until the game locked up or crashed. Auto-assignments now dispatch after the panel finishes rendering.

Fixed: the AI burned ~200 wasted actions (minutes of real time) whenever the roll-off winner wasn't the active player — deployment-order and first-turn choices are now submitted as the winning player.

releases.summary: First gamepad / Steam Deck foundations: menus are controller-navigable (D-pad moves focus, A selects, B backs out), the right stick pans the battle camera and the triggers zoom, a button-hint bar appears whenever a controller is used, and the UI Scale setting finally works.
changes: Gamepad basics: D-pad / left stick moves the UI focus and A presses buttons (B cancels) across the main menu and Settings — both are now fully usable without a mouse, and focus is handed back sensibly when the Settings overlay closes.
Controller camera: the right stick pans the board and LT/RT zoom out/in, at the same speeds as the WASD/=/- keyboard controls.
A hint bar at the bottom of the screen shows the controller buttons that do something right now; it appears when a controller is used and disappears the moment you go back to mouse and keyboard.
Fixed: the UI Scale slider (Settings → Visual) was saved but never applied — it now visibly resizes the whole interface, which will matter on small screens like the Steam Deck.
This is milestone M0 of the controller roadmap (PRPs/steam_deck_controller_support.md); board interactions (moving models, targeting) are still mouse-only until M1-M3.

releases.summary: AI thinking log now names duplicate squads with their Alpha/Beta/Gamma suffix, so you can tell WHICH 'Custodian Guard' the AI is reasoning about.
changes: All AI thinking/decision lines in the Game Log (movement plans, shooting picks, charge declarations, stratagem reasoning, etc.) now use each unit's full display name — e.g. 'Custodian Guard Alpha holds the objective' instead of the ambiguous 'Custodian Guard'.
Applies to both the AI's own squads and enemy squads it names as targets.

releases.summary: Duplicate squads that share a name (e.g. three 'Custodian Guard' units) are now tellable apart: the name label under each model — and the Movement/Charge/Fight unit lists — show the distinguishing Alpha/Beta/Gamma suffix instead of collapsing every one to an identical 'Custodian Guard ..'.
changes: On-board name labels now keep the disambiguating suffix. A long duplicate name like 'Custodian Guard Alpha' used to be cut to 'Custodian Guard ..' under every model, so you couldn't tell which models belonged to Alpha vs Beta vs Gamma — now the suffix is shown.
Very long names are shortened from the middle (e.g. 'Anointed Cust.. Epsilon') so the trailing squad suffix always stays visible.

The Movement, Charge and Fight unit-selection lists now show each duplicate squad's suffix (e.g. 'Custodian Guard Alpha', 'Custodian Guard Beta'), matching the Shooting list, so you can pick the right squad.

releases.summary: Fixed AI charges failing AFTER a successful charge roll ("charge move failed — skipping"). The AI now plans its charge move with the exact same shape-aware rules the game enforces — real oval/rectangular base shapes, walls, terrain climb costs, unit coherency and the 11e 'end within 1" if you can' rule — so a successful roll now actually lands the charge (e.g. Custodian Guard charging a packed Deffkoptas cluster).
changes: AI charge moves are no longer rejected after a successful roll. Against crowded targets on oval bases (e.g. Deffkoptas), the old placement approximated bases as circles and skipped several rules, so up to ~90% of tight charges were computed illegally, bounced off validation, and were skipped — after the log had already shown a successful charge roll.

The AI placement engine now uses the same true base shapes (including oval rotation), wall overlap, terrain climb penalties, unit coherency and non-target engagement-range checks as the game's charge validator, and honours the 11e rule that every model that can end within 1" of a target must do so.

Fixed the 11e 'must end within 1"' check demanding impossible endpoints: it could insist on a spot that overlapped a wall, ignored terrain climb costs, or (against oval bases) landed just outside its own 1" acceptance band — rejecting every legal move a player or AI made. It now only enforces endpoints that are themselves fully legal.
When a charge genuinely cannot be completed (e.g. the only path is blocked by walls), the AI now says so and fails the charge cleanly instead of submitting an illegal move first.

releases.summary: The Fight phase no longer jumps straight through the dice: melee attacks now resolve weapon by weapon with a visible pause after the hit roll and after the wound roll — just like staged shooting — including a Command Re-roll (1 CP) button on each die.
changes: Clicking Fight! now opens a Melee Resolution window that walks through each weapon in turn: the hit roll is shown and PAUSES ('Roll to Wound' button), then the wound roll is shown and pauses ('Continue to Saving Throws'), then saves resolve — so you can actually read what happened at every stage.
Command Re-roll (1 CP) is now available in melee: while paused after a hit or wound roll, click any die to re-roll it (once per phase, attacker's CP), exactly like the staged shooting flow.
Each weapon (e.g. Big choppa, Power klaw, and auto-included Extra Attacks weapons) resolves as its own step with a named 'Weapon X of Y' header and a running combat log inside the window.
A summary ('Melee attacks resolved — N enemy models destroyed') with a Close button ends the sequence; the next fighter selection then continues as before.
AI activations and multiplayer games keep the previous one-shot resolution (no pauses), matching how staged shooting behaves.

releases.summary: Made the online Save & Load screen far more reliable when the cloud server is slow or waking up. Instead of silently showing an empty 'Existing Saves' list (which looked like your saved games had vanished), it now retries automatically, shows a clear message if it still can't reach the server, and has a new ⟳ Refresh button to reload the list by hand.
changes: Fixed: on the itch.io / web build, opening Save & Load while the cloud server was still waking from idle could show NO saved games — the first request has to wake the sleeping server, and that attempt sometimes failed. The failure was silently drawn as an empty list, indistinguishable from 'you have no saves'. The game now automatically retries a few times (with a short, increasing delay) so a waking server recovers on its own.
New: a ⟳ Refresh button next to the Sort dropdown reloads the list of saved games from the server without reopening the dialog.

Improved: if the save list still can't be loaded, the box now says 'Couldn't reach the save server… press ⟳ Refresh to try again (your saved games are not lost)' instead of appearing empty. An empty account now clearly reads 'No saved games yet.'

Under the hood: cloud requests transparently retry on connection/timeout and 502/503/504 gateway errors (the signature of a cold-starting server), and a failed save-list fetch now reports a distinct error instead of masquerading as an empty result. (Note: if online saves seem to come and go, the server-side cause is documented for the maintainer in server/README.md — it must be fixed at the deployment, not in the game.)

releases.summary: Hold L to see lines of sight in ANY phase — solid green lines to enemies your units can see, dashed red to the ones they can't. Previously the L overlay only existed during the shooting phase (and even there usually drew nothing), so it looked broken. The left roster strip hotkey moved to B.
changes: Hold L: draws a sight line from each of your units to every enemy unit — solid green when a clear line of sight exists (drawn along the actual sighting line, base-edge aware), dashed red between the nearest models when the view is blocked. Works in every phase now, not just shooting.

With a unit selected (mover, shooter, charger or fighter), holding L shows just that unit's lines of sight, checked with the same rules the shooting phase uses for target eligibility (including 11e hidden/obscuring visibility).
In the shooting phase with a shooter selected, L still shows the detailed per-model debug view (sample points, blocked attempts, terrain highlights).
The overlay refreshes automatically while held, so lines follow models as they move and no longer fade out after a few seconds.
Left roster strip hotkey is now B (was L, which the LoS overlay always captured first, so it never worked); the '?' shortcut overlay lists both keys.

releases.summary: Fixed the AI hijacking your Heroic Intervention: at the end of the AI's Charge phase, your Heroic Intervention window now stays yours. Previously the AI answered it itself within a second — the log even claimed it used Heroic Intervention with YOUR unit (e.g. 'AI uses Heroic Intervention with Stompa') — and your dialog was declined for you before you could react.
changes: The Heroic Intervention offer at the end of the AI's Charge phase belongs to the defending player (you). The AI no longer evaluates it, no longer logs 'AI uses Heroic Intervention with <your unit>', and no longer auto-declines your window — the dialog now stays up for its full 5-second countdown and your choice is the one that counts.
The same protection covers your Fire Overwatch window during the AI's charges, and your Heroic Intervention charge move once you accept — the AI can never resolve those for you.

In AI-vs-AI games the defending AI can now actually use Heroic Intervention: its attempts previously always failed an internal rules check ('can only be used on your opponent's turn') and were silently declined.
The gold 'HEROIC INTERVENTION available' banner in the dice log now shows the edition's real cost (1 CP in 11th edition — it was hardcoded to 2 CP).

releases.summary: History view: jump straight between game-log steps — while viewing a past step you can now click any other log entry to show that one, no need to press Esc first.
changes: While viewing a past board state (after clicking a game-log entry), clicking a different log entry now switches the view directly to that step. Previously the read-only overlay silently swallowed clicks on the log, so you had to press Esc (Return to Live Game) before picking another step.
The game log stays fully usable while viewing history — scrolling the list and expanding card details work too, and the banner always names the step being shown.
Hardened the history board rebuild against two step clicks landing in the same frame, which could have created duplicate model tokens.

releases.summary: Reinforcement placement helpers are clearer: the red 'must be >9" from enemy models' no-deploy zone now shows around EVERY enemy cluster (not just the biggest one), and units arriving from Strategic Reserves now get a green band showing where they CAN be placed — within 6" of a board edge.
changes: Fixed: when placing a unit from Deep Strike or Strategic Reserves, the 9" exclusion bubbles now render around all enemy models. Before, only the single largest connected clump of enemies drew its no-deploy zone; other enemy groups elsewhere on the board were left unmarked, making it look like you could legally arrive next to them. Each separate enemy cluster now gets its own labelled '9" exclusion' zone.

New: units arriving from Strategic Reserves now show a green 'within 6" of edge' band around the board perimeter — the area where they are allowed to be set up. (Deep Strike units, which may arrive anywhere >9" from enemies, do not show this band.)

Under the hood: the enemy-exclusion overlay grouped models into connected clusters but only kept the largest merged shape after each step, silently discarding every other cluster; it now merges each cluster independently. The new Strategic Reserves band matches the placement validator exactly (model centre within 6" of the nearest board edge).

releases.summary: Fixed charge rolls being wrongly reported as failed when the roll would actually reach the enemy. A charging model only has to close to within engagement range (2"), not touch base-to-base — e.g. a 9" roll against a target 9.6" away now succeeds (it only needs to travel 7.6").
changes: Charge rolls that can reach a target's engagement range are no longer marked FAILED. Previously the after-roll target check compared the full base-to-base gap against the raw roll, ignoring the 2" engagement range — so a 9" roll against a target 9.6" away failed even though the model only needed to move 7.6" to get within 2".
The reachable ceiling for selecting charge targets is now 'roll + engagement range' (capped at the 12" declaration range) instead of just the roll, matching how the charge move itself is measured.
Genuinely out-of-reach charges still fail correctly (e.g. a 9" roll cannot reach a target 12" away, which needs 10").

releases.summary: Fixed the AI 'Fire Overwatch' popup covering your control buttons: when the AI reacts during your turn (e.g. firing Overwatch while you arrive a unit from Reserves), the AI Actions log now floats to the left of the right-hand panel instead of sitting on top of your Undo / Reset / Confirm buttons — so you can always undo or confirm a deployment.
changes: The AI Actions log (shown when the AI acts, including reactive Overwatch during your own turn) no longer overlaps the right-hand control panel. It now sits just left of that panel, over the board, so it never hides the reserve-deployment Undo / Reset Unit / Confirm Move buttons.
The overlay is also fully click-through now (a stray click on it always passes to the controls behind), so it can never intercept a button press even where it appears.

releases.summary: Fixed the Fight-phase pile-in 'ghost': moving a model during pile-in/consolidate no longer leaves a duplicate of it behind at its starting spot.
changes: Fight phase pile-in/consolidate: dragging a model no longer strands a copy of it at its original position. Previously a model could appear twice — once where it moved to and once where it started — so it looked like two overlapping models.

Under the hood: rapid board refreshes could rebuild the whole token set twice in the same frame, leaving an invisible duplicate token on every model; an interactive drag then moved only one of the pair and revealed the other as a 'ghost'. The refresh now ignores overlapping re-runs so each model keeps exactly one token.

releases.summary: Fixed first-turn handling when Player 2 wins the roll-off to go first: the battle round no longer advances after a single player's turn. Previously that made Player 1's very first turn count as Battle Round 2, so the movement phase wrongly let units arrive from Reserves / Deep Strike on turn one (the AI would 'suggest' bringing reserves in immediately).
changes: Reserves and Deep Strike units can no longer arrive on the first turn of the game when Player 2 takes the first turn — reinforcements are correctly gated to Battle Round 2 onward for both players.

The battle round now advances after the round's SECOND player finishes their turn (Player 1 when Player 2 went first), instead of always after Player 2 — so the game lasts the correct number of turns and ends on the right turn.

Start-of-round effects (per-round Command Point cap, kill counters, battle-round stratagem timing, Supply Drop objective removal, round-end autosave) now trigger on the correct turn when Player 2 goes first.
AI reserve/Deep Strike placement now reads the real battle round, fixing round-gated placement decisions.

releases.summary: Game-log step replay: click any entry in the game log to see the board exactly as it was at that moment. It's a read-only look back — the live game is never changed — with a banner and an Exit button (or Esc) to return.
changes: Click a line in the Game Log to revert the board tokens to how they looked at that step (deployments, moves, casualties, etc.).
The step you're viewing is highlighted in the log; a banner across the top names the step and the board is dimmed to show it's read-only.
Click another log line to jump to that step, or press the 'Return to Live Game' button (or Esc) to go back to playing — your live game is untouched.
Every game now records its steps in memory to power this (previously only AI-vs-AI games were recorded); the AI vs AI replay browser is unchanged.
History view is disabled in multiplayer games.

releases.summary: Fixed a Shooting phase soft-lock: after a unit threw a GRENADE, the shooting UI could get stuck — clicks did nothing, or 'Rolling dice…' showed with no result. Rejected shooting actions are now explained instead of failing silently.
changes: Throwing a GRENADE with the unit you were about to shoot with no longer strands the Shooting UI: re-selecting that (now spent) unit shows 'has already shot this phase' instead of building a dead weapon panel that swallowed every follow-up click.
Shooting actions that get rejected (assign target, confirm, grenade) now pop an explanation and re-sync the panel, instead of silently doing nothing after an optimistic 'Rolling dice…'.
The GRENADE stratagem is now correctly limited to once per phase — the button disappears after you use it (previously it could be offered again).
Auto-selecting the next shooter now skips units that can't shoot yet or have no targets in range, landing on a unit you can actually fire with.

releases.summary: Objective control fixes: a model whose base is only partly on a terrain-hosted objective now counts as holding it, and objective labels no longer claim 'CONTESTED' when nobody (or only one side) is in range — an empty marker now reads 'Uncontrolled'.
changes: Terrain-hosted objectives (11e): a model counts as within the objective as soon as ANY part of its base overlaps the hosting ruin area — previously only the model's centre point was checked, so a unit standing half on the objective was ignored and the marker wrongly showed CONTESTED.
Board labels, the Command panel and the Scoring panel now distinguish a genuine stand-off ('CONTESTED': both sides in range with equal, nonzero OC) from an empty marker ('Uncontrolled').
Sticky-objective locks (e.g. Get Da Good Bitz) and nearest-controlled-objective checks now use the same base-on-area rule as objective control on terrain-hosted objectives.

releases.summary: Fixed a Waaagh! bug: Ork units that Advanced during an active Waaagh! (or that have Full Throttle, like Stormboyz) can now declare charges in the Charge phase. The Waaagh!'s advance-and-charge and 5+ invulnerable save now correctly last until the start of your next Command phase instead of being wiped at the end of the Shooting phase.
changes: Units eligible to charge after Advancing (Waaagh!, Full Throttle, Kult of Speed's Adrenaline Junkies) are no longer refused a charge with 'advanced or fell back this turn' — the 11th-edition charge check now honours those abilities, matching the Charge-phase eligibility list.

Waaagh! effects (advance-and-charge, 5+ invulnerable save, and its other buffs) are no longer stripped at the end of the Shooting phase — they now persist until the start of your next Command phase as the rules intend, so an Ork unit that Advanced can still charge and keeps its invuln through the enemy's turn.

releases.summary: Disembark fixes: disembarked units no longer vanish from the board (previously they were left as invisible 'still embarked' ghosts marked Cannot Disembark), and the Combat Disembark option only appears when enemies are actually close enough to block a normal 3" set-up.
changes: Fixed: after placing all models of a disembarking unit, the models disappeared (leaving only blue coherency rings) and the unit was listed as 'Embarked in Transport — Cannot Disembark' even though the game log said it disembarked. The unit's embark state was being reset with the wrong empty value after the disembark completed.

The Combat Disembark (18.04) toggle in the disembark dialog now only appears when an enemy unit is near enough to the transport that setting up within 3" outside Engagement Range could actually be impossible — with no enemies nearby it no longer suggests the disembark will be a Combat Disembark.
Reworded the Combat Disembark toggle to make clear it is an opt-in mode ('Use Combat Disembark…'), and the disembark dialog no longer shows raw [color=…] markup in its text.

releases.summary: Fixed the charge-phase 'Snap to Contact' button doing nothing when clicked — it now moves every remaining charging model into base-to-base contact with the nearest charge target, sliding models around the target's base when a squadmate blocks the straight line.
changes: 'Snap to Contact' now works: previously the click silently errored out and no models moved, so charging units could never be one-clicked into base contact.

When several models charge the same target, the ones whose direct approach is blocked by an already-placed squadmate now sweep around the target's base to find the nearest legal base-contact spot instead of being left behind.
If a model genuinely has no legal spot within its charge distance, the info panel now says so and asks you to place that model by hand, instead of failing silently.

releases.summary: Stratagem unit pickers now tell duplicate squads apart: dialogs like GRENADE list 'Stormboyz Alpha / Beta / Delta' instead of three identical 'Stormboyz' buttons.
changes: The GRENADE stratagem dialog labels each eligible unit with its squad name (e.g. 'Warbikers Alpha' vs 'Warbikers Beta'), matching the names used in the game log and shooter list — you can now tell which squad you are picking.

Same fix everywhere a stratagem asks you to pick a unit: Fire Overwatch, Counter-Offensive, Heroic Intervention, Tank Shock targets, Deck Fraggers, Rolling Loot-heap, and the generic faction-stratagem unit picker.
Game-log entries and result popups for stratagems (grenade throws, tank shock, overwatch) also use the squad names, as do the Scatter, Sweeping Advance, Krump'n'Run and Acrobatic Escape prompts.

releases.summary: Engagement-range circles now match the rule. The red rings shown while moving were drawn at the old 1" range while the game (11th edition) enforces 2", so moves were refused when a model still looked clear of the circle — the rings, the fight-phase reach circles and the amber engaged rings are now all drawn at base edge + 2".
changes: Movement: the red no-go rings around enemy models are now drawn at the 11th-edition 2" engagement range instead of a hardcoded 1" — if your base stays off the red circle, the move is legal.
Fight phase: each fighter's orange engagement-reach circle now includes the model's own base radius, matching the base-edge-to-base-edge measurement the rules use.

The always-on amber 'engaged' rings now detect engagement base-edge to base-edge (like the rules engine) instead of centre-to-centre, size themselves to the anchor model's base, and ignore dead models.

releases.summary: You can now change the AI speed mid-game from the top menu: a new 'AI Speed' dropdown sits next to the AI Suggestion button with the same Fast / Normal / Slow / Step-by-step presets as the setup screen.
changes: New 'AI Speed' dropdown in the in-game top menu (next to AI Suggestion) — switch between Fast, Normal, Slow and Step-by-step at any time during an AI game.
The dropdown stays in sync with the existing < > adjust and / cycle hotkeys, the speed chosen on the setup screen, and loaded saves.
Picking Step-by-step from the dropdown pauses the AI before each action as usual — press Continue (Space) to advance.

releases.summary: Fight phase dialogs now have a single, clear confirm button: the redundant 'OK' button that duplicated 'Fight!' in the attack-assignment dialog (and did nothing useful in the unit picker) is gone.
changes: Assign Attacks dialog: removed the built-in 'OK' button that sat next to 'Fight!' and did the same thing — 'Fight!' is now the one button that confirms your weapon/target assignments and rolls the attacks.
Fight unit picker: removed its 'OK' button too — you pick a unit by clicking it, and 'OK' could only close the picker without selecting anything, stalling the fight sequence.

releases.summary: Fixed the board mouseover tooltip: it now works on every model anywhere on the board (not just a lucky few), and shows the unit's real name and stats instead of 'Unknown' with question marks.
changes: Hovering any model — yours or your opponent's — now reliably shows its unit tooltip regardless of camera pan/zoom (the hover check compared screen and board coordinates, so it only worked in one spot of the board).
Tooltip now shows real values (name, M/T/Sv/W/OC, invulnerable save, model count, wound totals) instead of 'Unknown' and '?' — it was reading fields that don't exist in the unit data.
Big bases (vehicles, monsters) are now hoverable across their whole base, not just the centre.
The tooltip stays on screen near the board edge and no longer pops up while the mouse is over the side/bottom panels.

releases.summary: Second new base army list: 'Custodes Lions' — a 2000pt Adeptus Custodes Lions of the Emperor force (3 Blade Champions, 6 Custodian Guard squads, Allarus Custodians, Prosecutors). The default matchup is now Recon Stomps (P1) vs Custodes Lions (P2).
changes: Added 'Custodes Lions' (Adeptus Custodes, Lions of the Emperor, 2000 pts) as a built-in army list: Blade Champion Warlord + 2 more Blade Champions, 6x5 Custodian Guard with guardian spears, 5 Allarus Custodians, and a Prosecutors squad.
Army pickers now default to the two newest lists: Recon Stomps for Player 1 and Custodes Lions for Player 2 — all other army lists remain available in the dropdowns.
Army lists now price repeat datasheets correctly (11e repeat pricing): the 2nd+ Blade Champion costs 125, the 4th+ Custodian Guard squad 225 — exported and built-in lists total their printed points.

releases.summary: New base army list: 'Recon Stomps' — a 2000pt Orks Speedwaaagh! force (Wazdakka Gutsmek, a Stompa, Deffkoptas, Warbikers, Stormboyz). It is now Player 1's default army.
changes: Added 'Recon Stomps' (Orks, Speedwaaagh!, 2000 pts) as a built-in army list: 2 Deffkilla Wartrikes (Kustom Shokk Box / Supa-burny Fuel), Mek (Dakkamek), Wazdakka Gutsmek (Warlord), 4x Stormboyz, 4x Warbikers, 2x Deffkoptas, 2x Gretchin, and a Stompa.
Both players can now pick the same army list: the second player's units are kept separate instead of silently replacing the first player's.
Cloud armies are no longer auto-selected over the default lists when the menu loads.

releases.summary: Both Adeptus Custodes detachments are now fully playable. Silent Hunters comes entirely from official 11th-edition data; Lions of the Emperor stratagems and enhancements use the official 10th-edition wording as a clearly-marked PROVISIONAL stopgap (the 11e dataset has not published their rules yet) — the same stopgap the Ork and Shield Host stratagems already use, and every such entry now says so in its rules text.
changes: Silent Hunters (all from 11e data): Deathsong Scythes (Vigilators melee gains [LANCE] and +1 Attack vs Psykers), Umbral Prosecution (Prosecutors ranged weapons gain [RAPID FIRE 2] and +1 AP), Synchronised Inferno (Witchseekers ranged weapons gain [BLAST]); detachment rule Skin-Crawling Disorientation (Anathema Psykana can perform mission actions after Advancing); enhancements Encircling Hunter (post-deployment redeploy of up to 3 Anathema Psykana Infantry units, Strategic Reserves allowed) and Psyk-out Grenades (unit gains the EXPLOSIVES keyword).

Lions of the Emperor stratagems (10e stopgap): Peerless Warrior (melee [PRECISION]), Gilded Champion (re-use a spent once-per-battle ability — not in the same phase, once per battle per model), Manoeuvre and Fire (shoot + charge after Falling Back), Defiant to the Last, Swift as the Eagle and Unleash the Lions all work and show their official 10e rules text.

Lions of the Emperor enhancements (10e stopgap): Superior Creation (first death: 2+ at end of phase returns the bearer with full wounds, outside Engagement Range), Praesidius (Lone Operative + Stealth), Fierce Conqueror (+2 melee Attacks per 5 enemy models within 6" of the bearer), and Admonimortis now uses its real 10e rule — +3 Strength, +1 AP and +1 Damage on the bearer's melee weapons (the old bearer-death mortal-wound trigger matched no edition's rules and was removed).

Every stratagem running on 10th-edition wording (War Horde, Shield Host and Lions of the Emperor) now carries a visible PROVISIONAL note in its rules text; it will be replaced automatically when the 11e dataset publishes the official rules.

Against All Odds (Lions detachment rule) fixes: the +1 to hit now also applies in melee (previously ranged only), and the buff is correctly limited to armies running the Lions of the Emperor detachment (Shield Host armies no longer benefit).
Stratagem-granted [LANCE] now actually adds +1 to wound on the charge (it previously set a flag nothing read) — this also fixes Servo-driven Charge (AdMech) and Plunging Talons (CSM).
Fixed: loading a save whose armies differ from the defaults restored the stratagem list but not the stratagem definitions, so faction stratagems failed with 'Unknown stratagem' after a restart.

releases.summary: AI units no longer get stuck on walls or sprint across the board after distant enemies — both AI armies play noticeably tighter.
changes: AI movement destinations are now checked against walls with the exact rule the game enforces, before the move is attempted. Wall-related move rejections dropped 99% in benchmarks (from ~49 per game to under 1), which un-stuck units that previously stood still for entire games (Caladius Grav-tank, Witchseekers, Lootas, Kommandos).

Only genuinely melee-focused units (Boyz, Warbosses, Meganobz...) now leave their objective assignments to hunt enemies — units with real guns fight from position instead of footslogging. One shared rule replaces three slightly different copies of this logic.

All hunters respect a chase cap (tunable MELEE_AGGRESSION_ADVANCE_THRESHOLD_INCHES, default 20"): enemies beyond any realistic move+advance+charge reach are ignored in favor of the mission. Previously units could chase targets 45+ inches away from round 1.

Benchmark validation: 10 more full AI-vs-AI games, zero stalls; Ork primary VP doubled again at pure defaults; Custodes play improved even more (their units were the most wall-stuck). Full analysis in tests/bench_baselines/2026-07-10_mechanism_fixes.md.

Known remaining gaps (documented, next up): the AI doesn't know how to move AIRCRAFT (the Wazbom Blastajet idles all game), and large hordes sometimes fail to find placement room around friendly vehicles.

releases.summary: The Ork AI now actually plays the mission: benchmark-driven tuning stopped its whole army chasing enemies across the board while objectives sat unclaimed.
changes: Diagnosed via 60 AI-vs-AI benchmark games: with faction aggression 1.8, 7-11 of ~14 Ork units chased enemies every round (round-1 chases at 45+ inches) — even shooty units like Lootas and Kaptin Badrukk — and Ork primary VP starved at ~6 per game with the army still alive.

Tuned Ork faction aggression from 1.8 to 1.2 (the exact value validated in A/B benchmark arms): truly melee-focused units (Boyz, Warbosses) still hunt, but mixed and ranged units now follow their objective assignments. Ork primary VP roughly tripled (~6 to ~21 per game) and the average loss margin vs Custodes at Hard halved (-28 to -15), producing the first Ork draw and win in benchmark history.
Measured first-turn advantage with a turn-swapped fixture: ~19 VP at Hard, ~9 at Normal. Combined with the tuning, disciplined Orks going first play the Custodes to rough parity.
All experiment tooling is committed and reusable: turn-swapped fixture, two Ork tuning profiles (tests/bench_profiles/), and three analysis reports under tests/bench_baselines/.

releases.summary: A 20-game AI-vs-AI benchmark soak found and fixed three ways games could freeze mid-battle, plus a wasted Ork heal.
changes: Fixed games freezing near the end of big movement phases: the AI's runaway-loop safety cap counted every model's move as an 'action', so armies with lots of models tripped it mid-phase and the game locked up (especially when the Painboss heal window opened at the same moment). The cap now only trips after 200 consecutive attempts that make no progress, and when it does trip it can resolve any pending window instead of freezing.

Fixed fight-phase stratagems being rejected when used during the opponent's turn (e.g. Orks using Unbridled Carnage in your fight phase) — the game validated them against whoever's turn it was instead of who was using them.
Fixed AI-vs-AI fights where the second AI player picked fight activations from the first AI's plan, which could silently end the fight phase early.
The Painboss's Sawbonez heal now actually heals: the AI was dropping the target model when using it, so the heal did nothing. It also now picks the most-wounded eligible model.
Committed the first AI benchmark baseline (tests/bench_baselines/): 20 games across Normal and Hard, all seeds now complete without stalls.

releases.summary: The AI now coordinates its movement phase like an army: units know what their squadmates are already doing, and the whole battle plan is narrated in the game log.
changes: Coordinated movement: units that already moved this phase count as 'en route' to their objective, so later units reinforce objectives that still need help instead of stacking onto the same one. Redirects are explained in the log ('obj_3 is already covered (Boyz en route) — redirecting to obj_4').

At the start of the AI's movement phase the game log shows its full army battle plan as its own card: one line per unit with its role (HOLD / MOVE / ADVANCE / SCREEN), target objective, distance and reason.

Each unit's detailed decision card (chosen option plus the top rejected alternatives with scores) now appears once, at the moment that unit acts — the log no longer repeats every unit's reasoning on every single action.
Fixed AI-vs-AI games where the second player each round silently played without its multi-phase plan (no charge intents, no 'lock the shooters' targets): the plan is now built per player.
Fixed AI reasoning leaking between decisions: a Command Re-roll card no longer swallows the previous decision's thinking lines.

The AI is now hand-tunable without touching code: edit data/ai_config.json (ships with the game) to override any of its ~90 scoring weights; per-player profiles and conditional rules layer on top. Full guide: docs/AI_TUNING.md. Also fixed profile rules firing in the wrong phase (a stale phase-name map meant 'MOVEMENT' rules ran during Scout).

Fixed AI-vs-AI games freezing in the fight phase: when a forced fight interrupted the global Consolidate step, the wrong AI kept trying to end the other player's half forever. The AI now always acts as whoever's half of the pile-in/consolidate step it actually is.
Fixed a script error in Bomb Squigs / Scatter targeting (wrong pixels-per-inch constant).

Windowed scenarios that let the AI play a turn are no longer flaky: the secondary-mission deck shuffle now respects the scenario's RNG seed, so the AI can't randomly draw a card that pauses it behind a human-only dialog mid-test.

releases.summary: Click a unit on the battlefield to select it in the Movement and Shooting phases — no more hunting for it in the right-hand list. If your current unit is mid-move or has targets staged, a 'Switch Unit?' popup asks before switching so nothing is lost by accident.
changes: Movement phase: clicking any of your units' models on the board selects that unit for movement, exactly like clicking its row in the unit list. Enemy models never steal your selection.
Movement phase: if the unit you were moving still has an unconfirmed move when you click a different unit, a 'Switch Unit?' popup offers to confirm that move and switch, or keep the current unit.

Shooting phase: clicking one of your units that can still shoot makes it the active shooter, same as picking it in the SELECT SHOOTER list. Clicking enemy models still assigns weapon targets as before.

Shooting phase: if you had manually staged weapon targets for the current shooter, the 'Switch Unit?' popup warns that switching discards them (and now really discards them — re-selecting the old shooter no longer resurrects stale assignments). Auto-assigned single-target defaults switch silently.
Quick-assign ('All → target') now counts as staging targets, so the switch confirmation protects it too.

releases.summary: Shooting fix: selecting a unit that has no eligible targets (everything out of range or out of line of sight — e.g. a boxed-in Stompa) no longer fails silently. The game now tells you why nothing can be shot.
changes: Selecting a shooter with no eligible targets now shows a warning toast, a red status line under the unit list, and a dice-log breakdown listing why each enemy unit can't be targeted (out of range, no line of sight, engagement, Lone Operative…).
Clicking an enemy model while such a unit is selected now explains why that enemy can't be shot instead of doing nothing.
Selecting a weapon when nothing is targetable now says so instead of asking you to 'click on an enemy unit'.

releases.summary: Army builder fix: an imported enhancement now adds its points to the list total (e.g. Kustom Shokk Box +10). The bundled Taktikal Brigade army's Warboss costs were also missing their enhancement points.
changes: Web army builder: importing a pasted list resolved enhancements but priced them at 0 — the roster total and the unit's points row now include the enhancement cost (re-derived from the dataset every reprice, so saved drafts heal too).
Bundled orks_taktikal army: the Warboss with Skwad Leader now costs 90 pts (75+15) and the Warboss with Mork's Kunnin' 95 pts (75+20); their stored costs had omitted the enhancements.

releases.summary: Every Ork detachment RULE now works too: Lissen 'Ere Taktiks (Taktikal Brigade), Try Dat Button! (Dread Mob), Turbo Boostas (Speedwaaagh!), Da Boss Is Watchin' (Bully Boyz), plus the passive rules — Mob Mentality, Dakka! Dakka! Dakka!, Adrenaline Junkies, Eager for the Fight and Thundering Wagons. The last two Enhancements (Gob Boomer, Press It Fasta!) come alive with them.
changes: Taktikal Brigade — Lissen 'Ere (Taktiks): in your Command phase each Boss Snikrot, Mek or Warboss can issue one of four Taktiks to a friendly ORKS unit within 6" — Get Stuck In (re-roll Charge rolls), Get On Wiv It (+1 Strength in melee), Sneaky Stalkin' (Stealth + Benefit of Cover for Infantry/Mounted, not Meganobz) or Shoota Drills (+1 to ranged Hit rolls for Infantry/Mounted). The issuer takes a Leadership test — failing it deals 1 mortal wound to the receiving unit but the Taktik still lands. One issue per boss and one Taktik per unit each battle round, never to Battle-shocked units; effects last until your next Command phase.
Gob Boomer enhancement now works: its bearer issues Taktiks to Orks Infantry/Mounted units within 18" instead of 6".

Dread Mob — Try Dat Button!: when one of your Mek, Orks Walker or Grots Vehicle units is selected to shoot or fight, roll a D6 for the Button Effect — SUSTAINED HITS 1, LETHAL HITS or +2 AP for that phase. Press It Fasta! now works: the bearer's unit rolls two dice and gets both effects.

Speedwaaagh! — Turbo Boostas: a Speed Freeks or Trukk unit (not Aircraft) can use its turbo when it Advances — no Advance roll, it moves a flat 24", its ranged weapons gain ASSAULT for the turn and it cannot charge. A new 'Use turbo' toggle appears in the Movement panel for eligible units. (The one-straight-line rider is not enforced yet.)

Bully Boyz — Da Boss Is Watchin': if your Waaagh! has been spent and a Warboss is still standing, you can call a SECOND Waaagh! once per battle — it only counts for Warboss, Nobz and Meganobz units. The Command panel offers 'DA BOSS IS WATCHIN' — WAAAGH AGAIN!' when it is available.
Green Tide — Mob Mentality: your BOYZ units now carry a 6+ invulnerable save, improved to 5+ while they have 10 or more models (refreshed each Command phase).
More Dakka! — Dakka! Dakka! Dakka!: your Orks (and Grots-crewed) units' ranged weapons count as ASSAULT, and gain SUSTAINED HITS 1 while your Waaagh! is active.
Kult of Speed — Adrenaline Junkies: Speed Freeks units can shoot and charge in a turn in which they Advanced or Fell Back.
Blitz Brigade — Eager for the Fight: your ORKS units re-roll Advance and Charge rolls in the turn they disembark from a Transport.
Rollin' Deff — Thundering Wagons: your Battlewagons, Kill Rigs and Hunta Rigs advance a flat 6" without rolling and re-roll Charge rolls.
Detachment keyword grants: Gretchin count as BATTLELINE in a Dread Mob army; Stormboyz count as BATTLELINE in a Taktikal Brigade army.

releases.summary: Every Ork detachment now has working Stratagems and Enhancements: Green Tide, More Dakka!, Bully Boyz, Da Big Hunt (with the Prey rule), Kult of Speed, Dread Mob, Blitz Brigade and Rollin' Deff join Speedwaaagh! and Taktikal Brigade.
changes: Green Tide: all six Stratagems (COME ON LADZ! model revival, BRAGGIN' RIGHTS, TIDE OF MUSCLE, BULLDOZER BRUTALITY 3" fight range, COMPETITIVE STREAK, GO GET 'EM! reactive move) and all four Enhancements (Raucous Warcaller counts-as-10, Bloodthirsty Belligerence, Ferocious Show Off, Brutal But Kunnin' bonus CP).

More Dakka!: all six Stratagems (CALL DAT DAKKA? shoot-back, SPESHUL SHELLS +1 AP within 18", ORKS IS STILL ORKS, GET STUCK IN LADZ! unit Waaagh!, HUGE SHOW-OFFS, LONG UNCONTROLLED BURSTS) and all four Enhancements (Da Gobshot Thunderbuss, Dead Shiny Shootas, Targetin' Squigs, Zog Off and Eat Dakka!).

Bully Boyz: all six Stratagems (ARMED TO DA TEEF, ALWAYS LOOKIN' FER A FIGHT, CRUSHING IMPACT, CUT' EM DOWN forced Desperate Escape, TOO ARROGANT TO DIE swing-backs, HULKING BRUTES) and all four Enhancements ('Eadstompa, Tellyporta Deep Strike, Da Biggest Boss +2W, Big Gob forced Battle-shock).

Da Big Hunt: the Prey detachment rule (auto-picked enemy MONSTER/VEHICLE/CHARACTER; Beast Snaggas re-roll charges against it and get +1 AP), all six Stratagems (DRAG IT DOWN crits on 5+ vs Prey, UNSTOPPABLE MOMENTUM mortal wounds, INSTINCTIVE HUNTERS into Reserves, STALKIN' TAKTIKS, DAT ONE'S EVEN BIGGA!, WHERE D'YA FINK YOU'RE GOING? reactive move) and all four Enhancements (Glory Hog Scouts 9", Proper Killy, Skrag Every Stash! sticky objectives, Surly as a Squiggoth).

Kult of Speed: all six Stratagems (SPEEDIEST FREEKS 5+/4+ invulnerable, SQUIG FLINGIN' Battle-shock at -1, BLITZA FIRE, DAKKASTORM, FULL THROTTLE!, MORE GITZ OVER 'ERE! reactive move) and all four Enhancements (Fasta Than Yooz charge after disembark, Speed Makes Right bonus CP, Squig-hide Tyres 6" consolidation, Wazblasta post-shooting move).

Dread Mob: all six Stratagems with the new push-it choice dialog (BIGGER SHELLS FOR BIGGER GITZ, DAKKA! DAKKA! DAKKA!, KLANKIN' KLAWS, EXTRA GUBBINZ -1 Damage, SUPERFUELLED BOILER, CONNIVING RUNTS) and three Enhancements (Gitfinder Googlez, Smoky Gubbinz, Supa-glowy Fing Command-phase zap). Press It Fasta! is listed but waits on the Try Dat Button! detachment rule.

Blitz Brigade: all six Stratagems (ARMOURED DUELLISTS, MOUNT UP LADZ end-of-fight embark, IMPERVIOUS, MEKANISED BRUTALITY, RUN 'EM DOWN, YOOZ IN TROUBLE NOW disembark-and-surge) and all four Enhancements (Runnin' Boots, Blitzkaptin vehicle redeploys, Supercharged Squig Oil, Tuff Git).

Rollin' Deff (provisional 11e rules from the community dataset): all five Stratagems (BRUTAL BROADSIDE Rapid Fire X, IMPENDING CRUNCH, DEVASTATING DRIFT Cleave 1, LONG UNCONTROLLED BURSTS, SPESHUL SHELLS 9") and all four Enhancements (Boarding Ramps, Targetin' Gizmos, plus the shared Dead Shiny Shootas and Da Gobshot Thunderbuss).
Freebooter Krew: all six Stratagems now show their real rules text and BASH AND GRAB is re-enabled.
Fixed: several Stratagems whose fine print mentions 'enemy units' (DED SNEAKY, DAT'S OURS, MOUNT UP LADZ and others) wrongly skipped the unit picker and did nothing while still spending the CP.
Fixed: the Scatter! reactive-move prompt could crash mid-check; distance checks now use the shared measurement helper.

releases.summary: Taktikal Brigade (Orks) is now playable: all six of its Stratagems and three of its four Enhancements work in-game, and the always-broken Stratagems panel button is fixed.
changes: All six Taktikal Brigade Stratagems now have their real rules text and working effects: FIGHT PROPPA (choose SUSTAINED HITS 1 or LETHAL HITS for a unit's melee weapons), KRUNCHIN' DESCENT (after a Stormboyz charge, D6 per model in Engagement Range — 4+ deals a mortal wound, max 6), DAT'S OURS (+1 Objective Control until the next Command phase), TAKTIKAL RETREAT (shoot and charge after Falling Back), ON TO DA NEXT (a free 6" move when an enemy Falls Back away from your mob — offered via the same dialog as Krump and Run), and DED SNEAKY (pull Kommandos or Stormboyz off the table into Strategic Reserves).
Skwad Leader enhancement: a Warboss with it can be attached to a Kommandos unit, and while leading them the unit deploys with Infiltrators and has Stealth.
Mek Kaptin enhancement: a Big Mek with it can be attached to a Flash Gitz unit, and the unit re-rolls Hit rolls on its ranged attacks.
Mork's Kunnin' enhancement: after deployment you can redeploy up to three ORKS units (any of them into Strategic Reserves).
Gob Boomer is listed but has no effect yet — it extends the Taktiks (Lissen 'Ere) detachment rule, which is not implemented (the same gap as Speedwaaagh!'s Turbo Boostas detachment rule).

Fixed the Stratagems panel ([S] button): clicking it did nothing because of a script error — it now opens, and unit-targeted faction Stratagems prompt you to pick the target unit instead of silently doing nothing.
Stratagem targeting is now enforced for faction Stratagems (correct keywords, not-yet-fought/shot, engagement-range conditions), and 'One X or Y unit' wordings are matched correctly.
Added a ready-to-play Taktikal Brigade army list (orks_taktikal) with Skwad Leader and Mork's Kunnin' equipped.

releases.summary: Fixed the AI 'AI Suggestion' hint (and any log entry added on its own) drawing on top of the existing game log instead of slotting in at the bottom.
changes: Asking the AI for a suggestion no longer covers earlier game-log entries: the new card is now appended at the bottom of the log where it belongs. Previously the card's slide-in animation left it stuck at the top overlapping older entries until the next thing was logged, which then bumped it into place.
This also fixes the same glitch for any single game-log entry added in isolation (not just AI suggestions).

releases.summary: Speedwaaagh! (Orks) is now fully implemented: all four of its Enhancements and all six of its Stratagems work in-game.
changes: Master Meknologist now improves the Ballistic Skill of the bearer's ranged weapons by 1 (e.g. a Big Mek's Shokk Attack Gun hits on 4+ instead of 5+).

Supa-burny Fuel now changes the Deffkilla Wartrike's killa jet weapons: the burna profile fires 3D6 Attacks and the cutta profile fires 3 Attacks. (Also taught the dice engine to roll multi-dice values like 3D6.)
Kustom Shokk Box: when the Deffkilla Wartrike turbos (Advances), it can move through dense terrain that would otherwise block it.
Dakkamek: when a Mek with this Enhancement uses its Mekaniak ability on a Vehicle, that Vehicle's ranged weapons gain [RAPID FIRE 1] until your next turn.

All six Speedwaaagh! Stratagems now work: ON DA MOVE (shoot/charge after Advancing or Falling Back), DUST TRAILS (Benefit of Cover), SPESHUL AMMO (Anti-Monster 4+ and Anti-Vehicle 4+ on non-Torrent ranged weapons), DED KILLY CONSTRUCTION (melee LANCE, plus +1 Damage if the unit charged), MOBILE DAKKASTORM (+2 Strength for your Speed Freeks / Trukk units against a marked enemy), and EVASIVE MANOOVA (pull a Speed Freeks / Trukk unit off the table into Strategic Reserves).

Two small faithful simplifications: MOBILE DAKKASTORM's marked enemy is chosen when you use the Stratagem rather than auto-picked from what was just hit, and Kustom Shokk Box applies on any Advance (turbo is not a separate action in-game).

releases.summary: The Stompa now fields on a proper 180mm round base. The dataset ships no base for it ('Use model'), so it was falling back to a tiny 32mm base.
changes: Building or importing an army with a Stompa now gives its model a 180mm round base instead of the 32mm default, so it deploys, blocks movement, and fights at the correct footprint.

Added a curated base-size override in the army-format converter for models the 40kdc dataset leaves as a dimensionless 'draft' base; the Stompa is the first entry. Added a converter test that fails if the Stompa ever regresses to the 32mm fallback.

releases.summary: New 'Abilities' filter in the game log groups the repeating passive-ability lines (e.g. 'Stand Vigil active', 'Guardian Eternal active') and hides them by default, so the log stays focused on what actually happened.
changes: Passive, always-on ability activations are re-checked at the start of every phase, so lines like "Custodian Guard ability 'Stand Vigil' active", "Telemon Heavy Dreadnought ability 'Guardian Eternal' active" and aura effects were repeated in the game log every turn. They are now all tagged as 'Abilities' — no matter which phase they fire in — instead of being scattered across the Move / Charge / Info buckets by chance wording in their description.

Added an 'Abilities' filter chip (teal 'A' badge) to the game-log filter bar. It is OFF by default, so these repetitive passive-ability lines no longer clutter the log. Open Filters and toggle 'Abilities' on any time you want to see exactly which passive abilities are active.
Only the repeating passive/aura activations moved under this filter — one-off actions you take (Waaagh!, Oath of Moment, stratagems, mission actions) stay in their normal categories.

releases.summary: Army Builder: the Orks 'Speedwaaagh!' detachment now offers its four Enhancements. They were missing from the dataset, so the builder showed no Enhancement option and imported lists silently dropped enhancements like Kustom Shokk Box.
changes: You can now add the Speedwaaagh! detachment's Enhancements in the browser Army Builder: Kustom Shokk Box (10 pts), Dakkamek (25 pts), Supa-burny Fuel (15 pts) and Master Meknologist (20 pts). Previously a Speedwaaagh! Character showed no 'Enhancement' dropdown at all.
Uploading/pasting a Speedwaaagh! list that includes one of these Enhancements now resolves it and keeps it (with its points) instead of dropping it as unresolved on import.
Added browser tests (unit + real-page end-to-end) that fail if these Enhancements ever go missing from the builder again.

releases.summary: Fix: the 'AI Suggestion' button now actually appears when you start a game against the AI (it was being created hidden and never shown).
changes: The 'AI Suggestion' button (and its K hotkey path) is now visible from the moment a human-vs-AI game starts. Previously the button was built after the code that reveals it had already run, so it stayed hidden for the whole game and the feature could not be used from the UI.
Added a regression test that starts a human-vs-AI game through the normal flow and fails if the button is not shown, so this cannot silently break again.

releases.summary: When playing against the AI you can now ask it for a suggestion on your own turn — a new 'AI Suggestion' button (hotkey K) shows what the AI would do and why, without taking the action for you.
changes: New 'AI Suggestion' button in the bottom HUD (and hotkey K) for games against the AI. Press it on your turn and the AI reasons about your current position — the phase you're in, your units, the enemy, the objectives — and posts its recommended action to the game log, e.g. 'Suggestion (Movement): Witchseekers advances aggressively toward Kommandos (28.0" away)'.

The suggestion is a full reasoning card: it shows the chosen action plus the alternatives it considered and rejected, each with a score, and (where it applies) highlights the options it weighed on the board. Great for learning the game, or for understanding and debugging how the AI thinks.

It only suggests — it never acts for you. Asking for a hint takes no game action, doesn't end your turn or phase, and has no effect on the AI opponent. The advice reflects the difficulty of the AI you're actually facing.
The button appears only in games with an AI opponent; the K hotkey is rebindable and listed in the shortcut overlay (press ? ).

releases.summary: Charges that declare more targets than the roll can reach are now clearly reported instead of showing a misleading 'SUCCESS', and the charge panel now tells you the roll needed to reach every target you've picked.
changes: A charge must end within Engagement Range of EVERY unit you declared it against. Previously the '2D6 = X vs Y needed — SUCCESS' line only measured the CLOSEST declared target, so declaring three units when your roll could only reach one still said SUCCESS — then the move silently failed and your unit ended up not in combat with anything, unable to fight. The roll result now checks ALL declared targets: if any is out of reach the charge is reported FAILED, naming the target(s) you couldn't reach and the distance you actually needed (e.g. 'FAILED — out of range: Shield-Captain; you needed to roll 7.9"').

New declaration-time hint: as you pick charge targets, the charge panel shows the 2D6 roll needed to reach ALL of them, calls out the farthest (hardest) target, and lists each target's distance — colour-coded from likely to impossible — so an out-of-reach target is obvious BEFORE you commit and roll.
